caption:
CSS :has() replaced 50 lines of JavaScript.

Parent selectors were impossible for 25 years.
Now it's one line of CSS.

5 real patterns you can use today.

📌 SAVE this — your JS files are about to get lighter.

Which pattern surprised you? Comment the number 👇

#css #webdev #frontend #javascript #coding #programming #html #design #uidesign #codingtips

slides.title: CSS :has( ) killed 50 lines of JS
slides.subtitle: The parent selector. 25 years in the making.

slides.title: Style a card that has an image
slides.desc: If the card contains an image, change its layout. No JavaScript class toggling.
slides.punchline: CSS reads the children. Styles the parent.

slides.title: Highlight a label when input is focused
slides.desc: The label lights up when its input gets focus. Zero JS event listeners.

slides.title: Hide empty sections automatically
slides.desc: If a container has no children, hide it. No JS isEmpty() check needed.
slides.punchline: CSS detects empty. CSS hides empty.

slides.title: Style forms with invalid fields
slides.desc: The submit button disables visually when any field is invalid. Pure CSS.
slides.punchline: Form validation UI with zero JavaScript.

slides.title: Dark mode without JS toggle
slides.desc: If a checkbox is checked anywhere on the page, switch the entire theme.
slides.punchline: A theme toggle in pure CSS. Really.

slides.title: CSS :has( ) patterns

items.label: :has(img)
items.desc: Style parent based on child content

slides.question: How much JS can you delete now? 👇

slides.teaser: Next: Container queries — the end of media queries
